title: Catalyst Watch: FedEx splits, tech CEOs hit Computex, Quantinuum IPO, and the jobs report
content:
[Wall Street New York stock exchange stock market]
alexsl

Welcome to Seeking Alpha's Catalyst Watch - a breakdown of some of next week's actionable events that stand out. Read more about the events that may impact stock prices next week.

MONDAY - JUNE 1

 	* VOLATILITY WATCH - Netflix (NFLX [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/NFLX]) and Globalstar (GSAT [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/GSAT]) have seen options volatility increase over the last week. The most overbought stocks per their 14-day relative strength index include Momentus (MNTS [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/MNTS]), Hyliion (HYLN [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/HYLN]), and Redwire (RDW [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/RDW]). The most oversold stocks per their 14-day Relative Strength Index include Verra Mobility (VRRM [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/VRRM]) and Suja Life (SUJA [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/SUJA]). Short interest is elevated on Kohl's (KSS [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/KSS]) after its +20% earnings move. 
 	* EARNINGS WATCH - Notable companies due to report include Hewlett Packard Enterprise (HPE [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/HPE]).
 	* DIVIDEND WATCH - Companies that have an ex-dividend date coming next week include McDonald's (MCD [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/MCD]), Halliburton (HAL [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/HAL]), Cigna (CI [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/CI]), and Western Digital (WDC [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/WDC]).
 	* IPO WATCH - Quantinuum (QNT [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/QNT]) headlines a busy week for IPOs. The Honeywell (HON [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/HON])-backed quantum computing platform is looking to raise up to $1.05B and to value it at roughly $12.7B. Other IPOs expected to price and begin trading include aerospace components manufacturer Applied Aerospace & Defense (AADX [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/AADX]), silver miner Sunshine Silver Mining & Refining (GATO), Quantinuum (QNT [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/QNT]), coastal property insurer Safepoint Holdings (SFPT [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/SFPT]), and gas energy firm Innio Holding (INIO [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/INIO]). The analyst quiet period ends on Hawkeye 360 (HAWK [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/HAWK]), Suja Life (SUJA [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/SUJA]), Rare Earths Americas (REA [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/REA]), and Odyssey Therapeutics (ODTX [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/ODTX]) to free up analysts to post ratings.
 	* DATA WATCH - Key data reports due to arrive during the first few days of the week include updates on monthly deliveries from Nio (NIO [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/NIO]), XPeng (XPEV [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/XPEV]), Li Auto (LI [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/LI]), and BYD Company (BYDDF [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/BYDDF]). Other reports of high interest to investors will be the monthly Macau gross gaming revenue release, the monthly update on Class 8 truck orders, firearm background check data from the FBI, and discount broker DARTs reports.
 	* ALL DAY - FedEx (FDX [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/FDX]) will officially split off its less-than-truckload trucking arm, FedEx Freight (FDXF [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/FDXF]), into a separate, publicly traded company while keeping the express and ground parcel businesses inside FedEx Corp. The move is structured as a tax-efficient spin-off. FedEx Freight will be a component of the S&P 500 Index right out of the gate.
 	* ALL DAY - The four-day Computex Taipei event will begin. The AI-first show includes a wall-to-wall lineup of chip CEOs, heavyweight forums on physical AI, and a dense calendar of launches spanning PCs, data centers, edge, and robotics. Qualcomm (QCOM [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/QCOM]) CEO Cristiano Amon, Intel (INTC [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/INTC]) CEO Lip-Bu Tan, Arm (ARM [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/ARM]) CEO Rene Haas, and Marvell Technologies (MRVL [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/MRVL]) CEO Matt Murphy will all give keynotes or major presentations. Nvidia is effectively co-branding its Computex presence as "NVIDIA GTC Taipei at COMPUTEX," running across the week of the show and CEO Jensen Huang delivers a high-profile keynote. Cisco (CSCO [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/CSCO]) also plans a big presence.
 	* ALL DAY - Snowflake Summit 26 next week will begin in San Francisco. Snowflake's (SNOW [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/SNOW]) four-day flagship conference in San Francisco will be focused on "Making AI Real for Business," with heavy emphasis on enterprise data, generative/agentic AI, and applications.
 	* ALL DAY - The Atlantic hurricane season officially begins. Traders expect more volatility than normal around crude oil futures this year if any major hurricanes develop near offshore drilling assets.

TUESDAY - JUNE 2

 	* EARNINGS WATCH - Notable companies due to report include Palo Alto Networks (PANW [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/PANW]), Dollar General (DG [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/DG]), Ulta Beauty (ULTA [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/ULTA]), Signet Jewelers (SIG [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/SIG]), and Yesway (YSWY [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/YSWY]), which will be reporting for the first time as a public company. Options trading implies double-digit swings for Oddity Tech (ODD [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/ODD]) and Victoria's Secret (VSCO [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/VSCO]) after their reports are released.
 	* ALL DAY - Federal Reserve Bank of Minneapolis President Neel Kashkari is scheduled to participate in a panel discussion at the 2026 Bank of Korea International Conference.
 	* ALL DAY - The Microsoft Build event will begin in San Francisco. The flagship developer showcase will kick off with a keynote from Microsoft (MSFT [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/MSFT]) CEO Satya Nadella.
 	* ALL DAY - The two-day Deutsche Bank Basic Materials Conference will begin. The conference will include a long list of presentations from senior executives across chemicals, mining, industrials, and related basic materials companies. 
 	* 8:30 A.M. Cleveland Federal Reserve Bank President Beth Hammack will give remarks and participate in a conversation on monetary policy.

WEDNESDAY - JUNE 3

 	* EARNINGS WATCH - Notable companies due to report include Broadcom (AVGO [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/AVGO]), Crowdstrike (CRWD [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/CRWD]), Five Below (FIVE [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/FIVE]), Medtronic (MDT [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/MDT]), and Macy's (M [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/M]). Options trading implies double-digit swings for Netskope (NTSK [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/NTSK]) and Petco Health and Wellness (WOOF [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/WOOF]) after their reports are released.
 	* ALL DAY - Honeywell (HON [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/HON]) will hold an Aerospace Investor Day event ahead of the planned spinoff of Honeywell Aerospace later in the year.
 	* 3:00 P.M. The Federal Reserve will release its Beige Book Report. The report will summarize current economic conditions across the Fed's districts.
 	* POSTMARKET - Costco (COST [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/COST]) will release its May sales report.

THURSDAY - JUNE 4

 	* EARNINGS WATCH - Notable companies due to report include Ciena (CIEN [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/CIEN]), Samsara (IOT [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/IOT]), Rubrik (RBRK [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/RBRK]), Docusign (DOCU [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/DOCU]), and Lululemon Athletica (LULU [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/LULU]). Options trading implies a double-digit swing for X-Energy (XE [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/XE]) and Planet Labs (PL [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/PL]) after they report.
 	* ALL DAY - Notable investor events include Radian Group's (RDN [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/RDN]) Investor Day, Solstice Advanced Materials' (SOLS [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/SOLS]) business update call, and Lucid Group's (LCID [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/LCID]) annual meeting.
 	* ALL DAY - Boralex (BRLXF [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/BRLXF]) (BLX:CA [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/BLX:CA]) shareholders will vote on the acquisition offer from Brookfield Asset Management (BAM [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/BAM]) (BEP [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/BEP]) (BN [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/BN]) and Caisse de Depot et Placement du Quebec.

FRIDAY - JUNE 5

 	* ALL DAY - The Manheim Used Vehicle Value Index will be released.
 	* ALL DAY - It is the one-year anniversary date of the Circle (CRCL [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/CRCL]) IPO.
 	* ALL DAY - The live-action _Masters of the Universe_ movie from Amazon MGM Studios and Mattel Studios is set to hit U.S. theaters. Mattel (MAT [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/MAT]) has a significant merchandising program tied to the film.
 	* ALL DAY - The Summer Game Fest conference will take place. The video game conference could generate buzz that is relevant to Sony's (SONY [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/SONY]) PlayStation, Nintendo (NTDOY [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/NTDOY]), Microsoft's (MSFT [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/MSFT]) Xbox, and major third-party publishers like Capcom, Square Enix, Bandai Namco, CD Projekt, and Epic Games.
 	* 8:30 A.M. The May jobs report will be released. Economists expect 93K job additions for the month and for the unemployment rate to remain at 4.3%.

_Seeking Alpha Editor Josh Fineman contributed to this article._

MORE ON THE MARKETS 

 	* RAMpocalypse: After Huge Rally, What I See Happening Next In The Chip Sector [https://seekingalpha.com/article/4909995-rampocalypse-after-huge-rally-what-i-see-happening-next-in-the-chip-sector]
 	* Blinded By Greed? [https://seekingalpha.com/article/4910001-blinded-by-greed]
 	* The Great Debt Debacle Has Arrived [https://seekingalpha.com/article/4909947-great-debt-debacle-has-arrived]
 	* The AI boom is creating jobs, not destroying them, Apollo says [https://seekingalpha.com/news/4598389-ai-boom-is-creating-jobs-not-destroying-them-apollo-says]

title: Quantinuum IPO said to be oversubscribed: report
content:
[3D illustration of a working quantum computer. Quantum computing concept]
adventtr

The initial public offering of quantum computing firm Quantinuum (QNT [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/QNT]) is oversubscribed by a double-digit multiple of the number of shares available, Bloomberg reported, citing people familiar with the plan.

Quantinuum is owned by industrial conglomerate Honeywell (HON [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/HON]), and its offering is set to occur early next month.

Honeywell and Quantinuum did not immediately respond to a request for comment from Seeking Alpha.

Quantinuum recently filed to offer 21.05M shares of its Class A common stock in its initial public offering and sees the price to be between $45 and $50 per share. At the top end of the range, Quantinuum would raise $1.05B and be valued at roughly $13B. The firm plans to apply to list its Class A common stock on the Nasdaq Global Market under the symbol “QNT.”

According to the filing, Quantinuum generated 2025 bookings of $79.3M, with revenue rising to $30.9M from $23.0M in 2024, while net loss widened to $192.6M as the company continued investing heavily in growth and commercialization efforts. Bookings were $1.3M for the three months ended March 31, 2026, compared to $1.9M for the three months ended March 31, 2025.

The firm recently signed [https://seekingalpha.com/news/4595839-honeywell-rises-as-quantinuum-unit-also-signs-deal-with-u-s-government] a deal with the U.S. federal government to receive R&D funding to address specific technology bottlenecks in the development of fault-tolerant trapped-ion quantum computers.

MORE ON HONEYWELL AND QUANTINUUM 

 	* Honeywell International Inc. (HON) Presents at Wolfe Research 19th Annual Global Transportation & Industrials Conference Transcript [https://seekingalpha.com/article/4906459-honeywell-international-inc-hon-presents-at-wolfe-research-19th-annual-global-transportation]
 	* Quantinuum Seeks IPO As It Continues Research-Heavy Development [https://seekingalpha.com/article/4904557-quantinuum-seeks-ipo-as-it-continues-research-heavy-development]
 	* Honeywell International Inc. 2026 Q1 - Results - Earnings Call Presentation [https://seekingalpha.com/article/4893893-honeywell-international-inc-2026-q1-results-earnings-call-presentation]
 	* Honeywell-backed Quantinuum seeks to raise $1.05B in IPO [https://seekingalpha.com/news/4596776-honeywell-backed-quantinuum-seeks-to-raise-105b-in-ipo]
 	* Honeywell rises as Quantinuum unit also signs deal with U.S. government [https://seekingalpha.com/news/4595839-honeywell-rises-as-quantinuum-unit-also-signs-deal-with-u-s-government]

title: Quantum Computing Stocks: Quantinuum IPO Valuation Versus IonQ, D-Wave
content:
Quantinuum, which has filed for an IPO, will likely start trading in early June as the number of quantum computing stocks continues to swell.

Continue Reading

title: Quantinuum IPO targets $12.7B valuation on Nasdaq
content:
Quantinuum Inc., the quantum computing unit majority-owned by Honeywell International Inc., is targeting a valuation of up to $12.7 billion in its U.S. initial public offering, according to Reuters.

Plans call for approximately 21.05 million shares to be offered at a price range of $45 to $50, with total proceeds expected to reach as much as $1.05 billion. Quantinuum intends to list on the Nasdaq Global Market under the ticker symbol "QNT," with J.P. Morgan and Morgan Stanley serving as joint lead active book-running managers.

Full-year 2025 results showed $30.9 million in revenue alongside a $192.6 million net loss; in the preceding year, the company had posted $23 million in revenue and a $144.1 million net loss. Revenue of $5.2 million and a net loss of $136.6 million were recorded in the first quarter ending March 31, 2026. As of March 31, 2026, Quantinuum held $677 million in cash and cash equivalents.

After the IPO closes, roughly 49.1% of combined voting power across both share classes will remain with Honeywell, which the filing says intends to continue as both a customer and partner going forward.

The IPO filing arrived shortly after Washington unveiled a $2 billion initiative — spread across nine quantum computing firms — aimed at keeping the United States competitive in the field; Quantinuum is slated to receive $100 million from that package, according to Bloomberg.

The company traces its origins to 2021, when a spin-out of Honeywell's quantum operations was combined with Cambridge Quantum to create Quantinuum. The company, led by CEO Rajeeb Hazra, develops full-stack quantum computing platforms for applications in fields including chemistry, financial services, pharmaceuticals, and cybersecurity. Customers and partners include JPMorgan Chase, Amgen, and Mitsui & Co.

Quantinuum's latest system, Helios, achieved 99.921% two-qubit gate fidelity with 98 physical qubits, which the company said is the highest among commercially available gate-based quantum systems. The company is targeting future systems called Sol, expected in 2027, and Apollo, expected in 2029.

Quartz previously reported that Quantinuum had filed a confidential draft registration statement with the SEC in February 2026, when the number of shares and price range had not yet been set. The company was valued at $10 billion in a September fundraising round, according to Reuters.

View Comments

title: Honeywell's Quantinuum's IPO puts the quantum stock rally to the test
content:
A Honeywell-backed (HON) company is aiming for one of the biggest public-market tests yet for the quantum computing trade.

Quantinuum is seeking to raise as much as $1.05 billion in its IPO, offering 21 million shares at $45 to $50 each. At the top of the range, Quantinuum would be valued around $12.7 billion and is expected to trade on Nasdaq (^IXIC) under the ticker QNT, according to IPO terms filed Tuesday.

That is a big ask for a company still in the early stages of commercializing quantum computing. Quantinuum was formed in 2021 through the combination of Honeywell Quantum Solutions and Cambridge Quantum, giving it both quantum hardware and software capabilities. The company says it is working toward a commercial-scale, fault-tolerant quantum computer before the end of the decade, with potential applications in chemistry, machine learning, cybersecurity, finance, and drug discovery.

Quantinuum reported $5.2 million of revenue in the March quarter, down from $19.1 million a year earlier. Its net loss widened to $136.6 million from $30.5 million over the same period.An inside look at an ion trap within Quantinuum's quantum computer, which processes data using trapped-ion technology, Broomfield, U.S. in this handout picture from 2019. Picture taken 2019. Quantinuum/Handout via REUTERS·Reuters / REUTERS

The swings show how early and concentrated the business remains. The filing says Japan’s RIKEN accounted for 90% of revenue in last year’s March quarter but just 7% in the latest period, while other government-linked customers made up much of this year’s revenue.

The company points investors instead to early demand and its long-term technology roadmap. Quantinuum reported $79.3 million of bookings in 2025, though bookings slowed to $1.3 million in the March quarter from $1.9 million a year earlier.

Its pitch rests on accuracy more than raw size. Quantinuum says its Helios system has 98 physical qubits, 48 logical qubits, and 99.921% two-qubit gate fidelity, a measure of how accurately quantum operations are performed. The company is targeting its next system, Sol, in 2027, followed by Apollo in 2029.

The IPO is landing against a friendlier backdrop for quantum stocks.

Public quantum names have rebounded hard since the broader market low on March 30, even after major drawdowns from their peaks.

IonQ (IONQ) is up about 132% since the end of March, while D-Wave Quantum (QBTS) is up about 110%. Rigetti Computing (RGTI) and Quantum Computing (QUBT) are both up more than 85%. Year-to-date gains are substantially lower.

But the rally has not erased the prior damage. Rigetti remains more than 50% below its record closing high, while D-Wave is still nearly 40% below its peak. IonQ is still down roughly 25% from its high, even after its recent rebound.

The sector also got a fresh policy boost this month, after the Trump administration announced quantum-computing funding tied to government equity stakes in several companies, including D-Wave and Rigetti.

Story Continues

Quantinuum has its own tentative government deal.

The company has a non-binding letter of intent with the Commerce Department for up to $100 million under the CHIPS Act, though the funding would come in tranches tied to milestones and Quantinuum would issue equity securities to the government in exchange.

Honeywell International (HON) is using that backdrop to unlock value while staying tied to the business. Honeywell will keep roughly 49% of the votes after the offering and plans to stay on as both a customer and a development partner.

That gives the deal a different feel from the already-public quantum names, many of which came to market through SPACs. Quantinuum is trying to become the sector’s serious IPO benchmark.

The question for investors is whether that cleaner quantum story deserves a premium price — or whether the sector’s rally has already pulled too much of the future into today’s valuation.

Jared Blikre is the global markets and data editor for Yahoo Finance. Follow him on X at @SPYJared or email him at jaredblikre@yahooinc.com.

That is a big ask for a company still in the early stages of commercializing quantum computing. Its net loss widened to $136.6 million from $30.5 million over the same period.An inside look at an ion trap within Quantinuum's quantum computer, which processes data using trapped-ion technology, in this handout picture from 2019. That roadmap is central to the IPO case because current revenue remains small.·Quantinuum SEC filing

The IPO is landing against a friendlier backdrop for quantum stocks.

Public quantum names have rebounded hard since the broader market low on March 30, even after major drawdowns from their peaks.

IonQ (IONQ) is up about 132% since the end of March, while D-Wave Quantum (QBTS) is up about 110%. Rigetti Computing (RGTI) and Quantum Computing (QUBT) are both up more than 85%. Year-to-date gains are substantially lower.

Story Continues

The company has a nonbinding letter of intent with the Commerce Department for up to $100 million under the CHIPS Act, though the funding would come in tranches tied to milestones, and Quantinuum would issue equity securities to the government in exchange.

title: Quantinuum targets $12.7 billion valuation in U.S. IPO
content:
Investing.com -- Honeywell's spinoff Quantinuum plans to raise up to $1.05 billion through a U.S. initial public offering that would value the quantum computing company at up to $12.7 billion, the firm announced on Tuesday.

The Broomfield, Colorado-based company intends to sell approximately 21,052,632 shares priced between $45 and $50 each. The proposed valuation represents an increase from the $10 billion valuation Quantinuum achieved in its most recent funding round.

The offering comes as new listings gain traction among investors backing sectors considered strategically important, including AI infrastructure, defense and critical technologies, amid geopolitical uncertainty.

Quantinuum was established in 2021 following its separation from Honeywell and subsequent merger with Cambridge Quantum. The company is chaired by Honeywell CEO Vimal Kapur and led by Intel veteran Rajeeb Hazra.

J.P. Morgan and Morgan Stanley serve as joint lead active book-running managers for the offering. Additional underwriters include BofA Securities, UBS Investment Bank, Jefferies, Evercore ISI, Cantor, Mizuho, Needham & Company, Societe Generale, TD Cowen, Craig-Hallum and Rosenblatt.

The company plans to list on the Nasdaq under the ticker symbol "QNT."

Related articles

Quantinuum targets $12.7 billion valuation in U.S. IPO

Morgan Stanley CIO survey: Why AI hype isn’t boosting 2026 IT budgets

title: Honeywell-Backed Quantinuum Seeks to Raise $1.05 Billion in IPO
content:
(Bloomberg) -- Quantinuum Inc., a quantum computing company backed by Honeywell International Inc., is seeking to raise $1.05 billion in its US initial public offering, capitalizing on investor enthusiasm for the technology.

Most Read from Bloomberg

Nvidia CEO Urges Super Micro to Tighten Up on Compliance Trump Doctor Visit Renews Health Scrutiny as 80th Birthday Nears Trump Says He’ll Announce Negotiated Deal With Iran Shortly Rubio Sees Good News Coming on Hormuz as Iran Talks Continue US, Iran Inch Toward Deal as Gaps Remain on Uranium, Sanctions

The company plans to market about 21 million shares for $45 and $50 each, according to its filing Tuesday with the US Securities and Exchange Commission. At the top of that range, Quantinuum would have a market value of $12.7 billion based on the outstanding shares listed in its filing.

Quantinuum makes powerful quantum computers that are capable of solving complex tasks beyond the abilities of traditional processors. Quantum computers have the potential to make exponential leaps in computing, necessary in artificial intelligence.

The company is developing platforms for use in fields such as chemistry, machine learning, cybersecurity, finance and drug discovery. It counts companies such as Amgen Inc. and Mitsui & Co. as early users and collaborators, the filing shows.

“We believe that we are executing a roadmap to the first commercial-scale, fully fault-tolerant quantum computer before the end of this decade, the Apollo system,” Chief Executive Officer Rajeeb Hazra wrote in a letter to investors included in the filing.

The Broomfield, Colorado-based company had a net loss of $136.6 million on revenue of $5.2 million for the three months ended March 31, compared with a net loss of $30.5 million on revenue of $19.1 million a year earlier, according to its filing.

The artificial intelligence frenzy that’s powered the broader market over the past three years stoked a rally in quantum computing stocks that reached a peak in October before seeing some selling pressure this year.

Shares of D-Wave Quantum Inc. surged more than 200% in 2025, extending a more than 800% rally from the previous year. Rigetti Computing Inc. shares are well off their October highs after surging nearly 3,000% from the start of 2023 through the end of last year.

The Trump administration is supporting the sector’s development, announcing on May 21 more than $2 billion in funding for a group of quantum computing firms in the US. Quantinuum is set to receive $100 million, while International Business Machines Corp. will get $1 billion, the largest award in the Commerce Department’s package.

Story Continues

The US government is set to receive minority, non-controlling stakes in the companies in exchange for the funding.

Quantinuum was formed in 2021, combining the hardware capabilities of Honeywell Quantum Solutions and Cambridge Quantum’s software. Honeywell has indicated its intention to remain a strategic customer and partner following the listing, per the filing.

In a list of potential risk factors, the company notes it will need to develop its manufacturing process to make computers in high volume, and that it currently relies on single-source suppliers for an array of materials and systems.

Three of Quantinuum’s four commercial quantum computing systems are located in its Colorado campus, the filing shows. The fourth is in Japan, and a fifth system is expected to be deployed in Singapore late this year.

JPMorgan Chase & Co. and Morgan Stanley are leading the offering. The shares are expected to trade on the Nasdaq Global Market under the symbol QNT.

--With assistance from Bre Bradham.

title: Honeywell's Quantinuum targets $12.7 billion valuation in US IPO
content:
May 26 (Reuters) - Honeywell's Quantinuum is targeting a valuation of up ‌to $12.7 billion in its U.S. initial ‌public offering, it said on Tuesday, as ​it looks to capitalize on heightened investor attention around quantum computing.

The Broomfield, Colorado-based company is planning to raise up to $1.05 ‌billion by selling ⁠about 21.05 million shares at $45 to $50 apiece. It had raised ⁠funds at a valuation of $10 billion in its latest funding round.

New listings ​are showing ​renewed momentum ​as investors back ‌sectors viewed as strategically important, including AI infrastructure, defense and critical technologies, despite geopolitical uncertainty.

Quantinuum, formed in 2021 after a separation from Honeywell and merger ‌with Cambridge Quantum, is ​chaired by the industrial ​giant's CEO, ​Vimal Kapur, and led ‌by Intel veteran Rajeeb Hazra.

J.P. ​Morgan ​and Morgan Stanley are the joint lead active book-running managers. Quantinuum will ​list on ‌the Nasdaq under the symbol "QNT."

title: Quantum Computing Stocks: Quantinuum IPO Filing Bucks SPAC Trend
content:
Honeywell's Quantinuum has filed for an IPO and, for a change, investors in quantum computing stocks may get more financial disclosure.
